prompt:
Photorealistic cinematic interior scene, soft directional studio lighting, quiet tense mood, depicting a craftsman seated at a wooden workbench in a dim workshop.

The craftsman’s hands are replaced by precise measuring instruments — calipers, rulers, and gauges — perfectly aligned and rigid, incapable of grasping or shaping anything.

The workbench is filled with unfinished objects — wood pieces, tools, and half-built forms — all carefully measured but clearly non-functional and incomplete.

The measuring instruments are perfectly calibrated and aligned, yet no actual creation is happening.

Composition: centered subject, slightly low angle, strong focus on the hands-instruments contrast, shallow depth of field blurring the background.

The viewer’s attention is drawn to the contradiction between perfect measurement and total inability to produce.

Mood: quiet frustration, sterile precision, conceptual failure.

Color palette: muted browns, cold metallic grays, soft shadows, subtle warm highlights.

No text, no numbers, no charts, no labels, no mathematical notation.

No literal targets, no dashboards, no performance indicators.
visual_logic: A craftsman with measuring tools as hands achieves perfect measurement but cannot create anything — optimization replaces function, showing how improving a measure destroys its usefulness as a measure.
